page: Microsoft Azure D96as_v5 is a general-purpose server belonging to the DASv5 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 96 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The processor operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and is supported by 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Benchmark evaluations indicate top-tier multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU encryption tests ranking in the top 10% of all tested servers. The system also exhibits strong performance in PassMark memory scoring and small-block cached memory bandwidth, both placing in the top 10% and top 25% respectively. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Geekbench multi-core scores, and software compilation with Clang also demonstrate strong top-25% tier performance. Large-block uncached memory read and write speeds remain in the average middle 50% range.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D96as_v5 offers high resource density and dedicated CPU allocation, making it a viable option for compute-heavy and memory-balanced enterprise workloads without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. It is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, software compilation, cryptography-heavy tasks, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D96as_v5 is a general-purpose server from the DASv5 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs across 48 physical cores, 384.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage or GPUs. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, it delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and encryption capabilities, ranking in the top 10% of benchmarks. It also shows strong top-25% performance in multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, and database operations. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance provides high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for demanding enterprise workloads. It is highly suited for database management, large-scale software compilation, cryptography, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96as_v5 is a general-purpose server featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 384 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for database and enterprise workloads.

tagline: A high-density, 96-vCPU general-purpose server delivering top-tier multi-core processing and balanced memory for demanding enterprise workloads.
